answer:
\boxed{C}  
Troponin T and Troponin I both rise and fall over a similar time course, but Troponin T tends to remain elevated slightly longer than Troponin I in myocardial infarction. Among the options, Troponin T is the marker that appears last and persists the longest. LDH and CPK-MB appear and clear earlier than the troponins.
